The psalmist was brought into great distress by a deceitful tongue. May every good person be delivered from lying lips. They forged false charges against him. In this distress, he sought God through earnest prayer. God can control their tongues. He received a gracious answer to this prayer. Surely sinners would not act as they do if they knew and would be persuaded to think about what will be the end of it all. The terrors of the Lord are his arrows; and his wrath is compared to burning juniper coals, which have intense heat and keep fire burning for a long time. This is the fate of the false tongue; for all who love and create lies will have their fate in the lake that burns forever.

It is very painful to a good person to be cast into, and kept in the company of the wicked, from whom he hopes to be separated forever. See here the character of a good person; he desires to live peacefully with all people. And let us follow David as he prefigured Christ; in our distress let us cry out to the Lord, and he will hear us. Let us pursue peace and holiness, striving to overcome evil with good.
